tools.name: list_actors
tools.description: List actors in the current level with optional filtering by class, name, tag, or folder. Returns actor names, classes, transforms, and tags.

tools.name: create_actor
tools.description: Spawn a new actor in the current level. Supports all standard UE actor classes including StaticMeshActor, PointLight, SpotLight, DirectionalLight, CameraActor, PlayerStart, etc.

tools.name: destroy_actors
tools.description: Delete one or more actors from the current level by their label names.

tools.name: set_actor_transform
tools.description: Set or modify the transform (position, rotation, scale) of an actor. Only provided fields are changed; omitted fields keep their current value.

tools.name: get_actor_properties
tools.description: Read UPROPERTY values from an actor. Returns property names, types, and values. Use without property_names to discover available properties.

tools.name: set_actor_property
tools.description: Set a UPROPERTY value on an actor. The value is provided as a string and parsed by the UE property system. Use get_actor_properties first to discover property names and current values.

tools.name: select_actors
tools.description: Select actors in the editor viewport by their labels. Useful for focusing on specific actors or preparing for batch operations.

tools.name: duplicate_actors
tools.description: Duplicate actors with an optional positional offset. Multiple copies can be created, each offset incrementally from the previous.

tools.name: set_actor_mobility
tools.description: Set the mobility of an actor's root component (Static, Stationary, or Movable).

tools.name: attach_actor
tools.description: Attach one actor to another as a child. The child actor will follow the parent's transform.

tools.name: detach_actor
tools.description: Detach an actor from its parent, making it a root-level actor again.

tools.name: get_actor_hierarchy
tools.description: Get the parent-child hierarchy for an actor: its parent (if any) and all directly attached children.

tools.name: set_actor_hidden
tools.description: Show or hide an actor in the editor viewport. Optionally propagates to attached children.

tools.name: set_actor_tags
tools.description: Add, remove, or replace tags on an actor. Default mode is 'replace' which overwrites all existing tags.

tools.name: focus_viewport
tools.description: Focus the editor viewport camera on a specific actor or world position. Provide either actor_name or x/y/z coordinates.

tools.name: set_viewport_camera
tools.description: Set the editor viewport camera position and rotation directly.

tools.name: take_screenshot
tools.description: Capture the current editor viewport as an image. Returns a base64-encoded JPEG. Useful for visual verification of scene state.

tools.name: get_selection
tools.description: Get the list of currently selected actors in the editor.

tools.name: undo
tools.description: Undo the last editor operation(s). Equivalent to Ctrl+Z.

tools.name: redo
tools.description: Redo the last undone operation(s). Equivalent to Ctrl+Y.

tools.name: run_console_command
tools.description: Execute an Unreal Engine console command. Useful for toggling debug visualizations, changing rendering settings, and more.

tools.name: list_assets
tools.description: List assets in the content browser with filtering by path, class, and name. Returns asset paths, classes, and file sizes.

tools.name: get_asset_info
tools.description: Get detailed metadata about an asset: class, package, tags, size, references, and key properties.

tools.name: import_asset
tools.description: Import a file from the filesystem into the content browser. Supports FBX, OBJ, PNG, JPG, WAV, and other standard formats.

tools.name: delete_asset
tools.description: Delete an asset from the content browser. By default checks for references to prevent breaking dependencies.

tools.name: duplicate_asset
tools.description: Create a copy of an existing asset at a new location with a new name.

tools.name: rename_asset
tools.description: Rename or move an asset to a new path. Automatically fixes up references.

tools.name: get_level_info
tools.description: Get information about the currently loaded level: name, path, actor count, world settings, and streaming levels.

tools.name: new_level
tools.description: Create and open a new empty level at the specified content path.

tools.name: open_level
tools.description: Open an existing level in the editor.

tools.name: save_level
tools.description: Save the current level and optionally all dirty packages.

tools.name: get_world_settings
tools.description: Read current world settings: gravity, kill-Z height, default game mode, navigation system class, and world bounds.

tools.name: set_world_settings
tools.description: Modify world settings for the current level. Set gravity, kill-Z height, default game mode, and world bounds checks. Only provided fields are changed.

tools.name: create_material
tools.description: Create a new Material asset with specified shading model and blend mode. The material is saved and ready for parameter editing.

tools.name: create_material_instance
tools.description: Create a Material Instance Constant from a parent material. Parameters can then be set using set_material_scalar/set_material_vector.

tools.name: set_material_scalar
tools.description: Set a scalar parameter value on a Material Instance Constant.

tools.name: set_material_vector
tools.description: Set a vector (color) parameter value on a Material Instance Constant.

tools.name: assign_material
tools.description: Assign a material to a static mesh actor's material slot. Works on any actor with a mesh component.

tools.name: set_static_mesh
tools.description: Set the static mesh asset on an actor's StaticMeshComponent. Works on any actor with a StaticMeshComponent.

tools.name: get_static_mesh_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a static mesh asset: vertex/triangle count, bounds, LOD count, material slots, and collision info.

tools.name: set_mesh_material_slots
tools.description: Batch-assign materials to all material slots on an actor's static mesh. Provide an array of material paths matching slot indices.

tools.name: create_static_mesh_actor
tools.description: Convenience tool: spawn a StaticMeshActor, set its mesh, and optionally assign a material — all in one call.

tools.name: configure_mesh_lod
tools.description: Configure LOD (Level of Detail) settings on a static mesh. Set the number of auto-generated LODs, screen size thresholds for each LOD transition, and triangle reduction ratio. LODs reduce rendering cost by showing simpler meshes at distance. Use get_mesh_complexity_report to see current LOD state.

tools.name: enable_nanite
tools.description: Enable or disable Nanite virtualized geometry on a static mesh asset. Nanite provides automatic LOD with virtually unlimited polygon counts. The mesh is saved after modification.

tools.name: get_mesh_complexity_report
tools.description: Get detailed complexity report for a static mesh: triangle count, vertex count, LOD count, screen sizes, Nanite state, material slot count, collision complexity, and estimated VRAM usage.

tools.name: batch_transform
tools.description: Apply the same transform change to multiple actors at once. Only provided fields are changed; omitted fields keep their current values.

tools.name: batch_set_property
tools.description: Set the same property value on multiple actors at once. Uses UE's property system for value parsing.

tools.name: find_actors
tools.description: Advanced actor query combining class, name, tag, and proximity filters. More powerful than list_actors for targeted searches.

tools.name: get_actor_bounds
tools.description: Get the world-space bounding box of an actor. Returns origin, extent, min/max corners, size, and center. The fundamental tool for understanding actor dimensions.

tools.name: get_mesh_asset_bounds
tools.description: Get the bounding box of a StaticMesh ASSET (before placing it in the level). Critical for calculating how many pieces span a distance or how things fit together.

tools.name: line_trace
tools.description: Cast a ray from A to B and report what it hits. Critical for finding ground level, checking line of sight, and placing on surfaces.

tools.name: overlap_test
tools.description: Check if an actor overlaps with anything at its current position, or test a hypothetical box at a position. Critical for collision-free placement.

tools.name: place_actor_on_ground
tools.description: Move an actor down (or up) to sit on the ground/surface below it. Traces straight down to find the surface, then positions the actor so its bottom sits on that surface.

tools.name: align_actors
tools.description: Align/snap actors relative to each other (min/max/center on any axis) or snap them all to a grid.

tools.name: stack_actors
tools.description: Stack actors on top of / next to each other with proper spacing based on their bounds. Critical for building walls, stacking crates, assembling modular pieces.

tools.name: measure_distance
tools.description: Measure distance between two actors or two points. Returns total distance, per-axis distances, and direction vector. Critical for spacing verification.

tools.name: get_spatial_context
tools.description: High-level spatial analysis of the current scene. Returns scene bounds, actor density, ground level, nearest actors, density map by quadrant, and empty spaces. Gives the AI a bird's-eye understanding of the scene layout.

tools.name: find_placement_position
tools.description: AI-friendly tool: find a good position to place something. Tries the desired position, checks overlap, and spirals outward to find clear space if blocked. Optionally snaps to ground.

tools.name: set_post_process_settings
tools.description: Set bloom, exposure, color grading, AO, and other post-process settings on a PostProcessVolume. Only provided parameters are changed.

tools.name: set_fog_settings
tools.description: Configure ExponentialHeightFog density, color, falloff, and volumetric fog settings.

tools.name: set_sky_atmosphere
tools.description: Configure SkyAtmosphere scattering, absorption, and height parameters for realistic sky rendering.

tools.name: set_light_properties
tools.description: Configure light properties: intensity, color, temperature, shadows, attenuation. Works on PointLight, SpotLight, DirectionalLight, and SkyLight actors.

tools.name: create_blueprint
tools.description: Create a new Blueprint class asset. Specify a content path and parent class. The Blueprint is saved and ready for editing.

tools.name: get_blueprint_info
tools.description: Get comprehensive information about a Blueprint: parent class, components, variables, functions, and event graph structure.

tools.name: add_component
tools.description: Add a component to a Blueprint's Simple Construction Script (SCS). The component will appear in the Blueprint's component hierarchy.

tools.name: set_component_property
tools.description: Set a default property value on a component in a Blueprint. Use get_blueprint_info first to discover component names.

tools.name: compile_blueprint
tools.description: Compile a Blueprint and report any errors or warnings.

tools.name: validate_blueprint
tools.description: Run pre-compilation validation on a Blueprint. Checks for orphan nodes (no connections), unwired execution pins, graph complexity, and reports warnings without compiling. Use before compile_blueprint to catch issues early.

tools.name: add_variable
tools.description: Add a new variable to a Blueprint with specified type and properties.

tools.name: spawn_blueprint
tools.description: Spawn an instance of a Blueprint class in the current level at the specified location.

tools.name: add_function_graph
tools.description: Create a new function graph in a Blueprint. Returns the function entry node ID. Use add_function_pin to add input/output parameters, and add nodes to build the function body.

tools.name: add_function_pin
tools.description: Add an input parameter or output return value to a Blueprint function. Input pins become function parameters; Output pins become return values. A FunctionResult node is automatically created if needed for outputs.

tools.name: add_function_return_node
tools.description: Add a FunctionResult (return) node to a function graph. Required for functions that return values. Use add_function_pin with direction 'Output' to add return value pins.

tools.name: add_event_node
tools.description: Add a built-in event node (BeginPlay, Tick, ActorBeginOverlap, ActorEndOverlap) to the event graph. Also accepts any UFunction name directly (e.g., 'ReceiveBeginPlay'). Returns the node ID for wiring.

tools.name: add_custom_event
tools.description: Add a custom event node to the event graph. Custom events can be called from other parts of the Blueprint. Returns the node ID.

tools.name: add_function_call_node
tools.description: Add a function call node to a Blueprint graph. Specify the target class and function name. Common classes: KismetSystemLibrary (PrintString, Delay), KismetMathLibrary (math ops), GameplayStatics (GetPlayerController, SpawnActor), Actor (SetActorLocation). Use 'Self' for Blueprint's own functions. Returns node ID and pin names for wiring.

tools.name: add_variable_get_node
tools.description: Add a variable getter (Get) node to a Blueprint graph. For self variables, omit target_class. For EXTERNAL class variables (e.g., getting a variable from BP_GameInstance or another Blueprint), provide the target_class name or content path. Returns node ID and output pin name for wiring.

tools.name: add_variable_set_node
tools.description: Add a variable setter (Set) node to a Blueprint graph. Has exec input/output pins and a value input pin. For EXTERNAL class variables, provide target_class. Returns node ID and pin names for wiring.

tools.name: add_branch_node
tools.description: Add a Branch (if/else) node. Has an exec input, a boolean 'Condition' input, and 'True'/'False' exec outputs. Returns node ID and pin names.

tools.name: add_switch_on_int_node
tools.description: Add a Switch on Integer node. Has an exec input, an integer 'Selection' input, a 'Default' exec output, and numbered case exec outputs. Returns node ID and all pin names.

tools.name: connect_pins
tools.description: Wire two Blueprint node pins together. Connect an output pin on one node to an input pin on another. The schema handles type checking and will report errors for incompatible types. Use get_node_pins to discover available pin names.

tools.name: disconnect_pin
tools.description: Break all connections on a specific pin of a node. Use get_node_pins to see current connections before disconnecting.

tools.name: set_pin_default_value
tools.description: Set the default (literal) value on an unconnected input pin. Use this to set parameter values like strings, numbers, booleans, vectors, etc. The value is parsed by the UE property system. For Vectors use 'X,Y,Z' format, for Rotators use 'P,Y,R' format.

tools.name: get_node_pins
tools.description: Get detailed pin information for nodes in a Blueprint graph. Returns pin names, directions (Input/Output), types, default values, and current connections. Essential for discovering pin names before using connect_pins. If node_id is omitted, returns all nodes with their pins.

tools.name: remove_node
tools.description: Remove a node from a Blueprint graph. All connections to/from the node will be broken. Cannot remove the function entry node of a function graph.

tools.name: add_make_struct_node
tools.description: Add a 'Make [Struct]' node that constructs a struct from individual member values. Works with any UScriptStruct: FVector, FRotator, FTransform, FLinearColor, FPostProcessSettings, FHitResult, etc. Returns node ID and all pins (one input per struct member + one struct output).

tools.name: add_break_struct_node
tools.description: Add a 'Break [Struct]' node that decomposes a struct into individual member output pins. Works with any UScriptStruct: FVector, FRotator, FTransform, FLinearColor, FPostProcessSettings, FHitResult, etc. Returns node ID and all pins (one struct input + one output per struct member).

tools.name: add_set_struct_fields_node
tools.description: Add a 'Set Members in [Struct]' node that sets individual fields of a struct. Unlike Make, this takes an existing struct as input and selectively modifies fields. Has exec pins (not pure). Use this for FPostProcessSettings, FHitResult, and other complex structs. Returns node ID and all pins (exec in/out, struct in/out, one input per struct member).

tools.name: add_for_each_loop_node
tools.description: Add a ForEachLoop macro instance node. Iterates over an array with 'Array Element' and 'Array Index' outputs, plus 'Loop Body' and 'Completed' exec outputs. Set with_break=true for the variant with a Break input. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_while_loop_node
tools.description: Add a WhileLoop macro instance node. Has a 'Condition' boolean input, 'Loop Body' exec output (runs while condition is true), and 'Completed' exec output (runs when condition becomes false). Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_delay_node
tools.description: Add a Delay node (latent action). Has exec input/output and a 'Duration' float input. The output exec fires after the specified duration. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_timeline_node
tools.description: Add a Timeline node to a Blueprint graph. Timelines allow you to animate float/vector/color values over time with keyframes. Has Play, PlayFromStart, Stop, Reverse exec inputs, Update/Finished/Direction exec outputs, and a float output for each track. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_cast_node
tools.description: Add a Cast To node (dynamic cast). Has an Object input, exec input, success/fail exec outputs, and a typed output pin for the cast result. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_spawn_actor_node
tools.description: Add a SpawnActorFromClass node. Has a Class input, SpawnTransform input, exec input/output, and a return value pin for the spawned actor. Optionally pre-fills the actor class. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_get_all_actors_of_class_node
tools.description: Add a GetAllActorsOfClass function call node. Returns an array of all actors of the specified class in the world. Has a WorldContextObject input, ActorClass input, and OutActors array output. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_sequence_node
tools.description: Add a Sequence node that executes multiple output pins in order. Has an exec input and numbered 'Then' exec outputs (Then_0, Then_1, ...). Useful for organizing sequential logic. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: list_class_functions
tools.description: List all callable Blueprint functions on a UClass. Essential for discovering exact function names before using add_function_call_node. Returns function name, whether it's pure/static, return type, and optionally full parameter details. Use name_filter to narrow results (e.g., name_filter='Print' on KismetSystemLibrary).

tools.name: get_function_signature
tools.description: Get the exact Blueprint node pin signature for a function. Returns all input and output pin names and types exactly as they will appear when using add_function_call_node + connect_pins. Use list_class_functions first to find the correct function name.

tools.name: add_event_dispatcher
tools.description: Add an event dispatcher (multicast delegate) variable to a Blueprint. Event dispatchers allow Blueprints to broadcast events that other Blueprints can bind to.

tools.name: add_call_dispatcher_node
tools.description: Add a 'Call' node for an event dispatcher. This broadcasts the event to all bound listeners. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_bind_dispatcher_node
tools.description: Add a 'Bind Event to Dispatcher' node. This binds a custom event to listen to the dispatcher. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: create_enum
tools.description: Create a UserDefinedEnum asset with optional initial entries. Entries can be added as a JSON array of strings.

tools.name: add_switch_on_string_node
tools.description: Add a Switch on String node. Has an exec input, a string 'Selection' input, a 'Default' exec output, and named case exec outputs. Returns node ID and all pin names.

tools.name: add_switch_on_enum_node
tools.description: Add a Switch on Enum node. Creates exec output pins for each enum value plus a Default pin. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: create_blueprint_interface
tools.description: Create a new Blueprint Interface asset. Blueprint Interfaces define function signatures that multiple Blueprints can implement.

tools.name: add_interface_to_blueprint
tools.description: Add a Blueprint Interface to an existing Blueprint, making it implement the interface's functions.

tools.name: get_blueprint_interfaces
tools.description: List all interfaces implemented by a Blueprint, including their names, paths, and function signatures.

tools.name: add_local_variable
tools.description: Add a local variable to a function graph. Local variables are scoped to the function and not visible outside of it.

tools.name: add_make_array_node
tools.description: Add a Make Array node that constructs an array from individual elements. Connect inputs to define array contents. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_flow_control_node
tools.description: Add a flow control macro node (DoOnce, FlipFlop, Gate, MultiGate, DoN). These are standard macro instances from the engine's StandardMacros library. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_select_node
tools.description: Add a Select node that picks one of several values based on an index or boolean input. Similar to a ternary operator. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_input_action_event
tools.description: Add an Enhanced Input Action event node to a Blueprint graph. This creates an event that fires when the specified InputAction is triggered. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: add_create_widget_node
tools.description: Add a CreateWidget node to a Blueprint graph. Creates a UMG widget instance at runtime. Has a Class input (set via widget_class), Owning Player input, exec input/output, and a Return Value pin (the created widget). Use with AddToViewport to display the widget. Returns node ID and all pins.

tools.name: execute_python
tools.description: Execute Python code in Unreal Engine's embedded Python environment. The 'unreal' module is available for accessing the engine API. Output is captured from the log. This is a powerful escape hatch for operations not covered by other tools.

tools.name: create_level_sequence
tools.description: Create a new LevelSequence asset at the specified content path. Sets the display frame rate and saves the asset immediately.

tools.name: open_sequence
tools.description: Open a LevelSequence asset in the Sequencer editor. The sequence will be focused and ready for editing.

tools.name: add_actor_to_sequence
tools.description: Bind an actor from the current level as a Possessable in a LevelSequence. The actor can then have tracks and keyframes added to it.

tools.name: add_sequence_track
tools.description: Add a track to an actor binding in a LevelSequence. Transform adds a 3D transform track; Visibility adds a bool visibility track. The actor must already be bound via add_actor_to_sequence.

tools.name: add_keyframe
tools.description: Add a keyframe at a specified time on a track belonging to a bound actor. For Transform tracks, the value is parsed as 'X Y Z' or 'X Y Z Pitch Yaw Roll' in world space. For Visibility tracks, the value is 'true' (visible) or 'false' (hidden).

tools.name: set_sequence_range
tools.description: Set the playback range (start and end times in seconds) of a LevelSequence. Times are converted to frame numbers using the sequence's tick resolution.

tools.name: play_sequence
tools.description: Open a LevelSequence in the Sequencer editor (if not already open) so it can be previewed. Playback must be triggered manually in the Sequencer UI after opening. This tool ensures the sequence is loaded and focused in the editor.

tools.name: get_sequence_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a LevelSequence: display rate, playback range, bound actors, track types, and section counts. Useful for inspecting existing sequences before modifying them.

tools.name: add_audio_track
tools.description: Add an audio track with a sound asset to an actor binding in a LevelSequence. The actor is automatically bound if not already present. The sound is placed at the start of the playback range.

tools.name: add_camera_cut_track
tools.description: Add a camera cut track to a LevelSequence pointing to a bound camera actor. Creates a CameraCut section spanning the specified time range. The camera actor must already be bound via add_actor_to_sequence.

tools.name: add_sub_sequence
tools.description: Embed a child LevelSequence inside a master LevelSequence using a Sub Track. The sub-sequence is placed at the specified time range on a new or existing Sub Track.

tools.name: add_fade_track
tools.description: Add a cinematic fade track to a LevelSequence. Creates a Fade track (master track) with keyframes for start and end fade values. 0 = no fade (clear), 1 = fully black. Useful for fade-in/fade-out transitions in cinematics.

tools.name: set_skeletal_mesh
tools.description: Set the skeletal mesh asset on an actor's SkeletalMeshComponent. Works on any actor that has a SkeletalMeshComponent (e.g., SkeletalMeshActor, Character). Wrap in an undo transaction.

tools.name: set_animation_blueprint
tools.description: Assign an Animation Blueprint to a SkeletalMeshComponent by loading the UAnimBlueprint asset, extracting its generated class, and calling SetAnimInstanceClass(). This determines how the skeleton is driven at runtime.

tools.name: play_animation
tools.description: Play a single UAnimSequence on an actor's SkeletalMeshComponent using AnimationSingleNode mode. This overrides any AnimBlueprint currently assigned. Useful for previewing animations directly in the editor viewport.

tools.name: get_skeleton_info
tools.description: Retrieve structural information about a skeletal mesh asset: total bone count, the first 50 bone names in hierarchy order, socket count, socket names, and the mesh bounding box. Useful for animation rigging and attachment workflows.

tools.name: list_animation_assets
tools.description: List UAnimSequence and UAnimMontage assets found in the Asset Registry under a given content path. Optionally filter by skeleton asset or name substring. Returns asset name, path, type (Sequence or Montage), duration in seconds, and frame count.

tools.name: get_landscape_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about landscape actors in the current level. Reports name, class, component counts, quad/vertex resolution, world bounds, scale, material, paint layers, and edit layers for each ALandscape and ALandscapeStreamingProxy. Useful for understanding existing terrain before making modifications.

tools.name: create_landscape
tools.description:
Create a new flat landscape actor in the current level using UE's standard import pipeline. The landscape is initialised with a flat (mid-grey) heightmap. Parameters control the component grid, subsection size, and world-space scale. Typical presets:
  Small  (1 km^2):  8x8  components, 1 section, 63 quads, scale 100
  Medium (4 km^2): 16x16 components, 1 section, 63 quads, scale 100
  Large  (8 km^2): 16x16 components, 2 sections, 127 quads, scale 100
After creation use get_landscape_info to confirm the result.

tools.name: set_landscape_material
tools.description: Assign a material to a landscape actor's LandscapeMaterial slot. The material should be a landscape-compatible material with layer blend nodes for paint layers.

tools.name: add_foliage_type
tools.description: Register a static mesh as a foliage type on the level's InstancedFoliageActor. If a foliage type for this mesh already exists it is returned unchanged. Use paint_foliage afterwards to scatter instances.

tools.name: paint_foliage
tools.description: Scatter foliage instances randomly within a radius around a world-space center. The foliage type is found or created automatically from the given mesh. A downward line trace finds the ground surface so instances sit correctly on terrain or geometry.

tools.name: erase_foliage
tools.description: Remove foliage instances within a spherical radius around a world-space center. If mesh_path is provided only instances of that foliage type are erased; otherwise all foliage types are affected.

tools.name: get_foliage_stats
tools.description: Return a summary of all foliage types registered in the current level, including the static mesh name and total instance count for each type. Returns an empty list when no InstancedFoliageActor exists in the level.

tools.name: get_world_partition_info
tools.description: Get World Partition configuration and status for the current level. Reports whether World Partition is enabled, data layers, and world bounds. Works with both World Partition and non-World Partition levels.

tools.name: load_world_partition_region
tools.description: Load World Partition editor cells within a bounding box region. Uses the console command 'wp.Editor.LoadRegion' to trigger cell loading. Requires World Partition to be enabled on the current level. Coordinates are in Unreal units (cm).

tools.name: spawn_niagara_system
tools.description: Spawn a Niagara particle system actor in the current level at a given world position. The system_path must point to a valid UNiagaraSystem asset in the content browser.

tools.name: set_niagara_parameter
tools.description: Set a user-exposed parameter on a NiagaraComponent attached to an actor. Use get_niagara_parameters first to discover available parameter names and types.

tools.name: get_niagara_parameters
tools.description: List all user-exposed parameters on a Niagara system, including their names, types, and any current override values set on the component.

tools.name: spawn_sound
tools.description: Spawn an AmbientSound actor in the current level at a given world position. The sound_path must point to a valid USoundBase asset (SoundWave or SoundCue) in the content browser.

tools.name: set_audio_properties
tools.description: Set audio component properties on any actor that has a UAudioComponent. Only provided parameters are modified. Use sound_path to swap the sound asset.

tools.name: get_sound_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a sound asset: type, duration, and for SoundWaves also channel count, sample rate, looping flag, and sound group.

tools.name: set_physics_simulation
tools.description: Enable or disable physics simulation on an actor's root PrimitiveComponent and configure physical properties: mass, damping, gravity, and per-axis translation/rotation locks.

tools.name: set_collision_profile
tools.description: Set the collision profile (preset) and/or collision enabled type on an actor's root PrimitiveComponent. Use profile_name for named presets like 'BlockAll' or 'PhysicsActor', or use collision_enabled for explicit control.

tools.name: add_physics_constraint
tools.description: Spawn an APhysicsConstraintActor that links two actors with a named constraint type. Fixed locks all motion, Hinge allows rotation on one axis, Prismatic allows sliding on one axis, BallSocket allows free rotation, Free allows all motion.

tools.name: get_physics_info
tools.description: Get a comprehensive physics report for an actor: simulation state, mass, damping, gravity, collision profile, bounds, velocity, center of mass, and inertia tensor.

tools.name: create_physics_material
tools.description: Create a PhysicalMaterial asset with configurable friction, restitution (bounciness), and density. Use assign_physics_material to apply it to actors.

tools.name: assign_physics_material
tools.description: Assign a PhysicalMaterial to an actor's root PrimitiveComponent. Controls friction, bounciness, and density for physics interactions.

tools.name: get_physics_material_info
tools.description: Read properties of a PhysicalMaterial asset: friction, static friction, restitution (bounciness), density, and surface type. Use to inspect existing physics materials before assigning them.

tools.name: list_collision_channels
tools.description: List all collision channels (default engine channels + custom project channels) with their default responses. Use with set_collision_response to configure per-actor collision.

tools.name: set_collision_response
tools.description: Set the collision response for a specific channel on an actor's root PrimitiveComponent. Use list_collision_channels to see available channels. Common channels: WorldStatic, WorldDynamic, Pawn, PhysicsBody, Vehicle, Destructible.

tools.name: build_navigation
tools.description: Trigger a navigation mesh build for the current level. Requires at least one NavMeshBoundsVolume in the level.

tools.name: query_navigation_path
tools.description: Find a navigation path between two world positions. Returns path points, total distance, and whether the path is complete.

tools.name: get_navigation_info
tools.description: Get navigation system information: navmesh bounds, agent settings, and build status.

tools.name: list_datatables
tools.description: List all DataTable assets in the project. Returns asset paths, row struct type, and row count.

tools.name: get_datatable_rows
tools.description: Read rows from a DataTable. Returns row names and all column values as JSON.

tools.name: add_datatable_row
tools.description: Add a new row to a DataTable. Provide column values as a JSON string with property names matching the row struct.

tools.name: create_user_struct
tools.description: Create a new UserDefinedStruct (Data Structure) asset. Optionally pre-populate with typed fields. The struct can be used in Blueprints, DataTables, and variables. Supported field types: Boolean, Integer, Float, String, Name, Text, Vector, Rotator, Transform, LinearColor, Object, SoftObject.

tools.name: get_struct_info
tools.description: Get detailed info about a UserDefinedStruct or any UScriptStruct: field names, types, and default values. Works for both Blueprint-created structs and engine structs.

tools.name: list_user_structs
tools.description: List all UserDefinedStruct assets in the project. Returns name, path, and field count for each struct.

tools.name: list_widget_blueprints
tools.description: List all Widget Blueprint (UMG) assets in the project. Returns asset name, content path, and parent class for each widget.

tools.name: spawn_widget_component
tools.description: Add a WidgetComponent to an existing actor for in-world UI display. The component can render a Widget Blueprint in 3D world space or screen space. Returns the component name on success.

tools.name: set_widget_component_property
tools.description: Set one or more properties on the first WidgetComponent found on an actor. Only provided fields are modified. Supports draw size, widget class, render space, tint color, interaction distance, and two-sided rendering.

tools.name: create_widget_blueprint
tools.description: Create a new Widget Blueprint (UMG) asset with a specified root panel type. The Widget Blueprint is saved and ready for editing with add_widget, set_widget_properties, etc.

tools.name: get_widget_tree
tools.description: Read the widget hierarchy of a Widget Blueprint. Returns a JSON tree showing all widgets, their types, names, children, and optionally their properties and slot configuration.

tools.name: add_widget
tools.description: Add a widget to a Widget Blueprint's widget tree. Supports all standard UMG widgets including layout panels (CanvasPanel, VerticalBox, HorizontalBox, etc.) and leaf widgets (Button, TextBlock, Image, etc.). Returns the created widget's name.

tools.name: remove_widget
tools.description: Remove a widget from a Widget Blueprint's widget tree by name. Also removes all children of that widget.

tools.name: set_widget_properties
tools.description: Set properties on any widget in a Widget Blueprint. Supports common properties (visibility, opacity, tooltip) and type-specific properties (text/font for TextBlock, color for TextBlock/Button/Image, percent for ProgressBar, size overrides for SizeBox, padding for Border). Only provided fields are modified.

tools.name: get_widget_properties
tools.description: Inspect a widget's current properties including type, visibility, opacity, and type-specific properties (text, colors, sizes). Also reports slot/layout information from the parent container.

tools.name: set_widget_slot
tools.description: Configure a widget's slot properties (positioning within its parent container). For CanvasPanel parents: set anchors, position offsets, alignment, z-order. For VerticalBox/HorizontalBox: set size rule (Auto/Fill), alignment, padding. For Overlay: set alignment, padding. Only provided fields are modified.

tools.name: move_widget
tools.description: Move a widget to a different parent in the widget tree, or reorder within the same parent.

tools.name: set_widget_image
tools.description: Set a texture on a UImage widget in a Widget Blueprint. Loads a Texture2D asset and assigns it as the image brush. Optionally set tint color and display size.

tools.name: bind_widget_event
tools.description: Create an event binding for a widget in a Widget Blueprint's event graph. Adds a K2Node_ComponentBoundEvent that fires when the specified widget event occurs (e.g., Button OnClicked, Slider OnValueChanged). The event node is placed in the event graph and can be wired to other nodes using Blueprint tools.

tools.name: batch_add_widgets
tools.description: Add multiple widgets to a Widget Blueprint in one call. Provide a JSON array of widget definitions, each with 'type' (required), 'name' (optional), and 'parent' (optional, defaults to root). Much faster than individual add_widget calls for building complex UIs.

tools.name: batch_set_widget_properties
tools.description: Set properties on multiple widgets in one call. Each operation specifies a widget name and the properties to set (same as set_widget_properties: text, font_size, visibility, render_opacity, is_enabled, tooltip_text, color_r/g/b/a, percent, width_override, height_override, justification). Much faster than individual calls.

tools.name: generate_ui_image
tools.description: Generate an AI image using fal.ai and import it into the UE content browser as a texture. Models: flux-2-flash (fastest, cheapest, supports transparency), nano-banana-2 (concept art), flux-pro (highest quality). Supports style presets and optional background removal via birefnet/v2. Requires fal.ai API key in Project Settings and PythonScriptPlugin.

tools.name: remove_background
tools.description: Remove background from an image using fal-ai/birefnet/v2. Provide a publicly accessible image URL. The result transparent PNG is imported into the UE content browser. Requires fal.ai API key and PythonScriptPlugin.

tools.name: generate_3d_model
tools.description: Generate a 3D model from a text prompt using fal.ai and import it into UE as a StaticMesh. Models: meshy-v6 (recommended, textured, supports PBR), meshy-v6-preview (faster/untextured), hunyuan-pro (highest fidelity up to 1.5M faces), hunyuan-rapid (faster), hunyuan-v3. Output is GLB format, imported via UE Interchange/glTF pipeline. NOTE: 3D generation takes 1-10 minutes depending on model. Requires fal.ai API key and PythonScriptPlugin.

tools.name: image_to_3d_model
tools.description: Generate a 3D model from an image using fal.ai and import it into UE as a StaticMesh. Models: trellis-2 (best quality, native 3D generation), meshy-v6-img (supports PBR/rigging/animation), hunyuan-v3-img, hunyuan-pro-img (high fidelity), rodin-v2 (production-ready), tripo-v2 (good stylized output). Provide a publicly accessible image URL. Output is GLB, imported via Interchange. NOTE: 3D generation takes 1-10 minutes. Requires fal.ai API key and PythonScriptPlugin.

tools.name: list_3d_models
tools.description: List all available fal.ai 3D generation models with their capabilities.

tools.name: list_pcg_graphs
tools.description: List all PCG Graph assets found in the project content browser. Optionally filter by path and/or name substring. Returns asset name and full content path for each graph.

tools.name: spawn_pcg_actor
tools.description: Spawn a new Actor with a UPCGComponent in the current level. Optionally assign a PCG Graph asset to the component. The actor's scale is set to the provided scale values, which determines the effective PCG volume bounds. Use execute_pcg after spawning to trigger graph generation.

tools.name: execute_pcg
tools.description: Trigger PCG graph generation on the UPCGComponent attached to the named actor. The component's assigned PCG Graph will be executed immediately with a forced regeneration. Use spawn_pcg_actor to create a PCG actor first, then call this tool to run the graph.

tools.name: get_pcg_info
tools.description: Retrieve information about the UPCGComponent attached to the named actor. Reports the assigned graph name and path, seed, generation trigger type, actor location and scale (which defines the effective generation bounds).

tools.name: create_pcg_graph
tools.description: Create a new UPCGGraph asset in the content browser at the specified path. The graph is saved immediately and registered with the asset registry. Use add_pcg_node to populate the graph with nodes after creation.

tools.name: get_pcg_graph_nodes
tools.description: List all nodes contained in a PCG Graph asset. Returns the node index, display title, settings class name, and editor position for each node. The node index can be used with add_pcg_node and connect_pcg_nodes.

tools.name: add_pcg_node
tools.description: Add a new node to a PCG Graph by specifying the UPCGSettings subclass to use. The node is appended to the graph and the package is marked dirty. Use get_pcg_graph_nodes to retrieve node indices for subsequent connect_pcg_nodes calls.

tools.name: connect_pcg_nodes
tools.description: Connect an output pin of one PCG node to an input pin of another PCG node in the same graph. Node indices correspond to the array order returned by get_pcg_graph_nodes. Default pin names 'Out' and 'In' are used when source_pin_label / target_pin_label are omitted. The graph package is marked dirty after a successful connection.

tools.name: set_pcg_static_mesh_spawner_meshes
tools.description: Assign one or more static meshes to a PCG Static Mesh Spawner node using the weighted mesh selector. Each mesh is added with equal weight. This is useful for completing PCG graphs created via MCP.

tools.name: create_spline_actor
tools.description: Spawn a new actor with a USplineComponent. Creates an initial spline with the specified number of points evenly spaced along the X axis.

tools.name: add_spline_point
tools.description: Add a point to an existing spline at the specified local-space position. If index is omitted the point is appended at the end.

tools.name: set_spline_point
tools.description: Modify the position and/or tangents of an existing spline point. Only provided fields are changed; omitted fields keep their current values.

tools.name: remove_spline_point
tools.description: Remove a spline point by index. Remaining points are re-indexed automatically.

tools.name: get_spline_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a spline: point count, total length, closed-loop state, and all point positions with tangents.

tools.name: set_spline_closed
tools.description: Set whether the spline forms a closed loop. When closed, the last point connects back to the first.

tools.name: set_spline_type
tools.description: Set the interpolation type of a spline point. Linear produces straight segments, Curve uses smooth Hermite interpolation, Constant holds the value, CurveClamped prevents overshoot.

tools.name: create_gameplay_ability
tools.description: Create a Blueprint inheriting from GameplayAbility (or a custom subclass). Requires the GameplayAbilities plugin to be enabled. The parent class is resolved dynamically by name.

tools.name: create_gameplay_effect
tools.description: Create a Blueprint inheriting from GameplayEffect. Optionally set the duration policy (Instant, Infinite, or HasDuration). Requires the GameplayAbilities plugin to be enabled.

tools.name: create_attribute_set
tools.description: Create a Blueprint inheriting from AttributeSet. Optionally pre-populate it with named Float variables representing gameplay attributes. Requires the GameplayAbilities plugin to be enabled.

tools.name: list_gameplay_abilities
tools.description: List all Blueprint assets that inherit from GameplayAbility. Searches the Asset Registry under the specified path. Requires the GameplayAbilities plugin to be enabled.

tools.name: list_gameplay_effects
tools.description: List all Blueprint assets that inherit from GameplayEffect. Searches the Asset Registry under the specified path. Requires the GameplayAbilities plugin to be enabled.

tools.name: list_attribute_sets
tools.description: List all Blueprint assets that inherit from AttributeSet. Searches the Asset Registry under the specified path. Requires the GameplayAbilities plugin to be enabled.

tools.name: add_ability_component
tools.description: Add an AbilitySystemComponent to a Blueprint's component hierarchy (SCS). The component class is resolved dynamically by name from the GameplayAbilities module. Requires the GameplayAbilities plugin to be enabled.

tools.name: get_gas_info
tools.description: Get Gameplay Ability System setup information for an actor in the current level. Reports whether the actor has an AbilitySystemComponent, lists granted abilities, and active gameplay effects. Requires the GameplayAbilities plugin to be enabled.

tools.name: create_input_action
tools.description: Create a UInputAction asset with a specified value type (Bool, Axis1D, Axis2D, Axis3D).

tools.name: create_input_mapping_context
tools.description: Create a UInputMappingContext asset for binding input actions to keys.

tools.name: add_action_mapping
tools.description: Add a key mapping to a UInputMappingContext, binding an InputAction to a specific key.

tools.name: list_input_actions
tools.description: List all UInputAction assets in the project. Returns asset names, paths, and value types.

tools.name: list_input_mapping_contexts
tools.description: List all UInputMappingContext assets in the project. Returns asset names and paths.

tools.name: get_input_mapping_info
tools.description: Get all key bindings in a UInputMappingContext. Returns action names, keys, modifiers, and triggers for each mapping.

tools.name: create_game_mode
tools.description: Create a new GameModeBase Blueprint. Optionally set the default pawn class. The GameMode controls match rules, spawning, and game flow.

tools.name: create_player_controller
tools.description: Create a new PlayerController Blueprint. The PlayerController handles player input, camera management, and HUD interaction.

tools.name: create_game_state
tools.description: Create a new GameStateBase Blueprint. The GameState holds replicated game-wide state such as scores, match timers, and team info.

tools.name: create_player_state
tools.description: Create a new PlayerState Blueprint. The PlayerState holds replicated per-player data such as player name, score, and team assignment.

tools.name: create_hud
tools.description: Create a new HUD Blueprint. The HUD class handles drawing canvas-based UI elements and managing the player's heads-up display.

tools.name: get_game_framework_info
tools.description: Get the current world's game framework configuration. Returns the GameMode class and its configured default pawn, player controller, player state, HUD, and game state classes.

tools.name: create_behavior_tree
tools.description: Create a new BehaviorTree asset at the specified content path. The tree is saved empty and ready for editing.

tools.name: create_blackboard
tools.description: Create a new BlackboardData asset at the specified content path. The blackboard is saved empty and ready for key editing.

tools.name: add_blackboard_key
tools.description: Add a key to an existing BlackboardData asset. Supports Bool, Float, Int, String, Vector, and Object key types.

tools.name: get_behavior_tree_info
tools.description: Get information about a BehaviorTree asset: root node class, linked blackboard, and node count.

tools.name: get_blackboard_info
tools.description: Get information about a BlackboardData asset. Returns all defined keys with their names and types.

tools.name: list_ai_assets
tools.description: List AI-related assets (BehaviorTrees, BlackboardData, EnvQuery) found in the project content browser.

tools.name: create_eqs_query
tools.description: Create a new Environment Query System (EQS) query asset at the specified content path.

tools.name: set_bt_blackboard
tools.description: Link a BlackboardData asset to a BehaviorTree. The behavior tree will use this blackboard for its AI context data.

tools.name: create_basic_level
tools.description: Creates a complete basic level with all essentials: floor plane, DirectionalLight, SkyLight, SkyAtmosphere, PostProcessVolume (infinite extent), ExponentialHeightFog, PlayerStart, and NavMeshBoundsVolume. Configures lighting based on preset (Day/Night/Sunset/Indoor).

tools.name: create_trigger_volume
tools.description: Creates a TriggerBox actor with configurable position, extents, and optional tag. Useful for creating gameplay trigger zones.

tools.name: create_light_rig
tools.description: Creates a 3-point lighting setup (key, fill, rim) around a center position. Key light is front-left, fill light is front-right (softer), rim/back light is behind the subject. All lights placed in 'Lighting/LightRig' folder.

tools.name: create_grid_layout
tools.description: Creates a grid of StaticMeshActors arranged in rows and columns. All actors are placed in a 'Layout/Grid' folder.

tools.name: create_ring_layout
tools.description: Creates a circular arrangement of StaticMeshActors evenly distributed around a ring. Optionally rotates each actor to face the center. All actors placed in 'Layout/Ring' folder.

tools.name: create_staircase
tools.description: Creates a staircase from N copies of a mesh arranged in ascending steps. Each step is offset by step_height vertically and step_depth horizontally along the yaw direction. All actors placed in 'Layout/Staircase' folder.

tools.name: get_project_info
tools.description: Returns comprehensive project information: project name, engine version, target platforms, build configuration, source modules, content paths, and general project settings.

tools.name: list_project_modules
tools.description: Lists all modules in the project (game modules + plugin modules). Shows module name, type, loading phase, and associated plugin if any.

tools.name: get_build_configuration
tools.description: Returns current build configuration: debug/development/shipping, target platform, compiler settings, and key preprocessor defines.

tools.name: validate_assets
tools.description: Validates assets in a given content path, checking for broken references and missing dependencies. Returns a list of assets with issues and their broken dependency details.

tools.name: get_map_check_errors
tools.description: Runs a map check on the current level and returns errors and warnings. Reports issues such as actors with NULL references, missing meshes, lighting build status, and other common level problems.

tools.name: build_lighting
tools.description: Trigger a lighting build for the current level. Quality levels: Preview (fast, low quality), Medium, High, Production (slow, best quality). The build runs asynchronously — use get_lighting_build_info to check progress.

tools.name: get_lighting_build_info
tools.description: Check the current lighting build status, quality, and whether the level needs a lighting rebuild. Reports lighting-related warnings.

tools.name: create_anim_blueprint
tools.description: Create a new Animation Blueprint (UAnimBlueprint) asset targeting a specific skeleton. Optionally specify a parent class derived from UAnimInstance. The created asset is saved to disk immediately and ready for graph editing.

tools.name: get_anim_blueprint_info
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about an existing Animation Blueprint: parent class, target skeleton, anim graph names, state machine names, montage slot groups referenced, and sync groups. Useful for understanding an AnimBP structure before modifying it.

tools.name: create_blend_space
tools.description: Create a 2D UBlendSpace asset targeting a specific skeleton. Configure the X and Y blend axes with display names and value ranges. After creation, use add_blend_space_sample to populate the grid with animation samples.

tools.name: add_blend_space_sample
tools.description: Add an animation sequence as a sample point to an existing BlendSpace at the specified (X, Y) coordinates. The coordinates must fall within the axis ranges defined when the BlendSpace was created. Multiple calls can be used to populate the blend grid with different animations.

tools.name: create_aim_offset
tools.description: Create a UAimOffsetBlendSpace asset targeting a specific skeleton. Aim offsets are specialised 2D blend spaces designed for additive aiming poses. The X axis defaults to horizontal aim angle [-90, 90] and Y axis to vertical aim angle [-90, 90]. Use add_blend_space_sample to add additive AnimSequence poses to the grid.

tools.name: create_anim_montage
tools.description: Create a new UAnimMontage from an existing animation sequence. The animation is placed into a named slot track which determines which slot on the AnimGraph is driven. The created montage can be played on a character at runtime using PlayAnimMontage() or Montage_Play().

tools.name: get_anim_montage_info
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about an AnimMontage asset: total duration, composite sections with their start times and next section links, slot track names with their animation segments, and anim notify events. Useful for understanding or debugging a montage before runtime playback.

tools.name: list_anim_assets_by_skeleton
tools.description: List all animation assets (AnimSequence, AnimMontage, BlendSpace) that use a specific skeleton. Uses the Asset Registry to enumerate assets efficiently without loading them all. Returns asset name, path, type, and for sequences their duration and frame count.

tools.name: add_anim_notify
tools.description: Add an animation notify event at a specific time on an AnimSequence or AnimMontage. Notifies trigger gameplay events like footstep sounds, weapon hit detection, combo windows, and VFX. The notify is added as a simple Blueprint-dispatchable notify.

tools.name: list_anim_notifies
tools.description: List all animation notifies on an AnimSequence or AnimMontage with their trigger times, names, types, and track indices.

tools.name: create_anim_state_machine
tools.description: Add a new state machine node to an Animation Blueprint's AnimGraph. State machines manage animation states and transitions (e.g., Idle→Walk→Run→Jump). Returns the machine node ID for use with add_anim_state.

tools.name: add_anim_state
tools.description: Add a new state to an existing state machine in an Animation Blueprint. Each state represents an animation state (Idle, Walk, Run, etc.). Use add_anim_transition to connect states. Use get_anim_state_machine_info to see existing states.

tools.name: add_anim_transition
tools.description: Add a transition rule between two states in an Animation Blueprint state machine. The transition controls how and when the animation blends from one state to another. Use get_anim_state_machine_info to see available states.

tools.name: get_anim_state_machine_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about all state machines in an Animation Blueprint: state names, transitions, default state, and connected animations. Use this to understand the structure before modifying.

tools.name: add_material_expression
tools.description: Add a material expression node to a UMaterial asset's expression graph. Specify the class by its short Unreal name (e.g. 'MaterialExpressionAdd'). Returns the zero-based index of the newly created expression for use in subsequent connect/set calls.

tools.name: connect_material_expression
tools.description: Connect a material expression's output pin to another expression's input pin or to a material output slot (BaseColor, Metallic, etc.). Set target_expression_index to -1 to connect to a material output; target_input_index then selects the slot: 0=BaseColor 1=Metallic 2=Specular 3=Roughness 4=EmissiveColor 5=Opacity 6=Normal 7=WorldPositionOffset.

tools.name: set_material_expression_value
tools.description: Set a default value or property on a material expression node by index. Supports setting scalar constants (Const), per-channel vector values (ConstR/ConstG/ConstB/ConstA), vector parameter defaults (DefaultValue as 'R,G,B,A'), and parameter names (ParameterName).

tools.name: get_material_expressions
tools.description: List every expression node in a UMaterial's graph with its index, class name, editor graph position, and connected input information. Use the returned indices with add/connect/set/remove tools.

tools.name: add_texture_sample_expression
tools.description: Convenience tool: add a UMaterialExpressionTextureSample node to a material with a specific texture asset already assigned. Returns the new expression's index for use in connect_material_expression.

tools.name: add_material_parameter_expression
tools.description: Add a scalar, vector, or texture parameter expression to a material. Parameter expressions are exposed when creating material instances, allowing per-instance value overrides without recompiling. Returns the new expression's index.

tools.name: remove_material_expression
tools.description: Remove a material expression node at the given index. Any connections referencing this expression will be cleared automatically. WARNING: Removing an expression will shift the indices of all subsequent expressions. Use get_material_expressions to verify current indices before calling.

tools.name: compile_material
tools.description: Trigger a full recompile of a UMaterial by calling PreEditChange + PostEditChange. Call this after making expression graph changes (add/connect/set/remove) to apply them. The material will be saved with pending changes marked dirty.

tools.name: create_metasound_source
tools.description: Create a new MetaSound Source asset at the specified content path. The MetaSound plugin must be enabled in Edit > Plugins. The asset is saved to disk immediately after creation.

tools.name: get_metasound_info
tools.description: Return information about a MetaSound asset: asset name, class, output format (channels), duration, and looping flag. Properties are read via UObject reflection.

tools.name: set_metasound_parameter
tools.description: Set a default parameter value on a MetaSound asset using UObject property reflection. The parameter_name must match a UPROPERTY on the MetaSound class. Use get_metasound_info to discover the asset's class and then consult the MetaSound documentation for available properties (e.g., 'OutputFormat', 'bLooping').

tools.name: list_metasound_assets
tools.description: List all MetaSound Source and MetaSound Patch assets in the project using the Asset Registry. Returns name, content path, and class for each asset found.

tools.name: duplicate_metasound
tools.description: Duplicate an existing MetaSound Source or Patch asset to create a variant. Uses IAssetTools::DuplicateAsset so that all internal MetaSound graph data is properly deep-copied. The duplicate is saved to disk immediately.

tools.name: set_metasound_quality
tools.description: Set quality and output settings on a MetaSound Source asset using UObject reflection. Supports looping, duration, and output channel count (mapped to the OutputFormat enum). Only parameters that are explicitly provided are modified.

tools.name: get_replication_info
tools.description: Get a full report of the network replication settings on an actor: bReplicates, bReplicateMovement, bAlwaysRelevant, bOnlyRelevantToOwner, NetUpdateFrequency, MinNetUpdateFrequency, NetPriority, NetDormancy, and the current net role.

tools.name: set_replication_settings
tools.description: Configure network replication settings on an actor. Only the parameters you provide are applied. Changes are wrapped in an undo transaction. Use get_replication_info to inspect the current state before modifying.

tools.name: set_net_dormancy
tools.description: Set the network dormancy mode on an actor to control how aggressively replication bandwidth is conserved. Dormant actors stop sending replication updates until manually woken with FlushNetDormancy. Use DORM_DormantAll for static or infrequently-updated actors to save bandwidth.

tools.name: get_component_replication
tools.description: Get a list of all components on an actor with their replication state. Reports: component name, class, bIsReplicated, and bReplicateUsingRegisteredSubObjectList for each component.

tools.name: set_component_replication
tools.description: Enable or disable network replication on a specific component of an actor. The actor must have replication enabled (bReplicates) for component replication to have any effect at runtime. Use get_component_replication to list component names.

tools.name: search_engine_class
tools.description: Search installed Unreal Engine headers for a class definition and extract its declaration with members. Searches the actual installed engine source, providing accurate API info for the current engine version. Returns file path, line number, and class body.

tools.name: search_engine_api
tools.description: Grep-like search through installed Unreal Engine headers. Finds methods, properties, macros, and any text patterns in the engine source. Useful for discovering API signatures, checking method existence, and finding usage patterns.

tools.name: get_engine_header
tools.description: Read the contents of a specific Unreal Engine header file. Use search_engine_class or search_engine_api first to find the file path. Supports reading specific line ranges for large files.

tools.name: add_gameplay_tags
tools.description: Register new Gameplay Tags in the project's tag hierarchy. Tags use dot-separated paths (e.g., 'Character.State.Stunned', 'Weapon.Type.Rifle'). Parent tags are automatically created. Tags persist in the project's DefaultGameplayTags.ini.

tools.name: list_gameplay_tags
tools.description: List all registered Gameplay Tags in the project. Filter by substring or parent tag to narrow results. Returns tag names in hierarchical dot-notation.

tools.name: set_actor_gameplay_tags
tools.description: Assign Gameplay Tags to an actor via its Tags property. Tags must already be registered (use add_gameplay_tags first). Modes: 'add' appends, 'remove' removes specific tags, 'replace' overwrites all tags.

tools.name: create_control_rig
tools.description: Create a new Control Rig Blueprint for a specific skeleton. Control Rigs provide IK chains, bone constraints, and procedural animation. Requires the ControlRig plugin to be enabled. The rig is created via the execute_python bridge for maximum compatibility.

tools.name: get_control_rig_info
tools.description: Get information about a Control Rig Blueprint: preview mesh, rig hierarchy, bone count, and available controls. Requires the ControlRig plugin.

tools.name: create_folder
tools.description: Create a new folder in the content browser. Creates all intermediate directories if needed. Does nothing if the folder already exists.

tools.name: move_assets_to_folder
tools.description: Bulk move multiple assets to a destination folder with automatic reference fixup. Creates the destination folder if it doesn't exist.

tools.name: get_asset_size_report
tools.description: Report the largest assets in the project by disk size. Useful for finding optimization targets. Filter by class to focus on textures, meshes, etc.

tools.name: get_asset_references
tools.description: Get the dependency graph for an asset. 'dependencies' shows what this asset depends ON (textures, materials, meshes it uses). 'referencers' shows what OTHER assets reference this one. Use to understand impact before modifying or deleting.

tools.name: find_unused_assets
tools.description: Find assets that are not referenced by any other asset in the project. These are candidates for cleanup/deletion. Note: some assets may be referenced at runtime via soft references or data tables.

tools.name: import_asset_with_settings
tools.description: Import an asset file (FBX, OBJ, PNG, TGA, WAV, etc.) into the project with detailed import settings. For FBX: control mesh type, material/texture import, mesh combining, and animation import. For textures: control compression and sRGB. Uses Unreal's automated import pipeline.

tools.name: set_texture_settings
tools.description: Modify texture settings after import: compression type, sRGB, max resolution, LOD group, and mipmap generation. Only provided fields are changed. The texture is re-saved after modification.

tools.name: create_state_tree
tools.description: Create a new StateTree asset. State Trees are UE5's modern replacement for Behavior Trees — more flexible, data-driven, and performant. Requires the StateTree plugin to be enabled. Uses the Python bridge for maximum compatibility.

tools.name: get_state_tree_info
tools.description: Get information about a StateTree asset. Uses Python bridge for compatibility. Reports states, transitions, evaluators, and tasks.

tools.name: add_state_tree_state
tools.description: Add a new state to a StateTree asset. States can contain tasks (what to do), transitions (when to move), and child states. Uses Python bridge for compatibility. Use get_state_tree_info to inspect the tree before modifying.

tools.name: set_state_tree_evaluator
tools.description: Configure an evaluator or condition on a StateTree state. Evaluators compute values each tick, and conditions gate transitions. Uses Python bridge. Common evaluators: StateTreeCompareIntCondition, StateTreeCompareFloatCondition, StateTreeCompareEnumCondition.

tools.name: list_state_trees
tools.description: List all StateTree assets in the project. Returns asset name and content path.

tools.name: create_common_ui_widget
tools.description: Create a Widget Blueprint using CommonUI base classes. CommonUI provides optimized cross-platform UI with built-in input routing, gamepad support, and action bindings. Requires the CommonUI plugin. CommonActivatableWidget = screens/panels. CommonButtonBase = buttons with input actions.

tools.name: configure_common_button
tools.description: Configure a CommonButtonBase widget with input actions, styles, and interaction behavior. CommonButtons support gamepad/keyboard navigation, input action bindings, and selectable (toggle) mode. Uses Python bridge for property access.

tools.name: set_common_ui_input_mode
tools.description: Configure CommonUI input routing mode. Controls whether the UI responds to mouse, gamepad, or both input methods. Uses Python bridge. 'Mouse' = mouse/keyboard only, 'Gamepad' = gamepad/keyboard only, 'All' = all input methods active.

tools.name: list_common_ui_widgets
tools.description: List Widget Blueprints that use CommonUI base classes (CommonActivatableWidget, CommonButtonBase, etc.).

tools.name: get_render_stats
tools.description: Get rendering performance statistics for the current viewport: estimated draw calls, triangle count from visible static meshes, light count by type, shadow caster count, and actor distribution by class.

tools.name: get_memory_report
tools.description: Get memory usage report: system memory stats, and disk size of assets by category (Textures, StaticMeshes, Blueprints, Materials, Animations, Audio). Shows total size and top N largest assets per category.

tools.name: profile_actors_in_view
tools.description: Profile per-actor rendering cost for actors in the current viewport frustum. Returns actors sorted by estimated render cost: triangle count, material count, shadow casting, Nanite state, and component count. Use to identify performance bottlenecks.

tools.name: create_scene_from_template
tools.description: Create a pre-configured scene from a template. Combines floor, lighting, sky, post-processing, and template-specific elements. Templates: fps_arena (closed arena with cover), tps_playground (open area with obstacles), rpg_outdoor (landscape with trees), horror_interior (dark room with fog), empty_studio (clean lighting setup for showcasing).

tools.name: search_project
tools.description: Fuzzy search across the entire project: assets, Blueprint functions, variables, and level actors. Supports partial names, CamelCase fragments, and approximate matching. Use this when you don't know the exact name or path of something. Results are ranked by relevance score (lower = better match).

tools.name: rebuild_search_index
tools.description: Force rebuild the project search index. Call this after significant project changes (importing assets, creating Blueprints, adding actors) to ensure search results are up to date. The index auto-builds on first search if empty.

tools.name: query-blueprint-summary
tools.description: Return a compact Blueprint summary with counts for graphs, functions, variables, and components.

include_names.description: Include function, variable, and component names

tools.name: query-blueprint-graph-summary
tools.description: Return compact graph summaries for a Blueprint, with optional sample node handles.

include_sample_nodes.description: Include a small sample of node handles

tools.name: query-blueprint-node
tools.description: Return detailed information for a specific Blueprint node, including object/class pin defaults.

tools.name: query-animation-asset-summary
tools.description: Return compact animation asset summaries for AnimSequence, AnimMontage, BlendSpace, and AnimBlueprint assets.

tools.name: query-level-summary
tools.description: Return a compact list of actors in the current level or world, with optional filtering.

tools.name: query-actor-detail
tools.description: Return detailed reflected information for a specific actor in the editor or PIE world.

include.description: Optional detail sections: metadata, hierarchy, tags, visibility, mobility, bounds, components, properties

tools.name: query-actor-selection
tools.description: Return the current actor selection for the resolved world, with optional transform and bounds data.

include_transform.description: Include actor transforms in selection results

include_bounds.description: Include actor bounds in selection results

tools.name: query-spatial-context
tools.description: Return spatial context for a set of actors, including bounds, aggregate bounds, pairwise distances, and optional ground hits.

items.description: Actor reference using either actor_name or actor_handle

actor_handle.description: Actor handle returned by query-level-summary or query-actor-selection

include_ground_hits.description: Include ground-hit traces below each actor

trace_distance.description: Ground trace distance in world units

tools.name: query-world-summary
tools.description: Return high-level world state, including actor counts, loaded levels, and current selection.

tools.name: query-material-summary
tools.description: Return compact material parameter summaries with optional value expansion, true parameter filtering, and optional graph overview for base materials.

asset_path.description: Material or material instance asset path

include_graph_overview.description: Include compact expression graph overview for UMaterial assets

tools.name: query-material-instance
tools.description: Return material instance parent info and explicit parameter overrides.

tools.name: query-static-mesh-summary
tools.description: Return compact static mesh summaries including bounds, material slots, LOD settings, Nanite state, and per-LOD triangle counts.

tools.name: query-environment-summary
tools.description: Summarize editor environment lighting actors including directional lights, sky lights, sky atmosphere, exponential height fog, and post process volumes.

tools.name: query-navigation-state
tools.description: Query navigation system state for the target world, including registered nav data and bounds volumes.

include_bounds.description: Include bounds for nav data and bounds volumes

tools.name: query-replication-summary
tools.description: Query Blueprint replication settings for class defaults and authored member variables.

tools.name: get-project-info
tools.description: Get project and plugin information including project name, path, and plugin version. Optionally include project settings (input mappings, collision, gameplay tags, maps).

section.description: Include settings section: 'input', 'collision', 'tags', 'maps', or 'all'. Omit for basic info only.

tools.name: query-asset
tools.description: Query assets: search by pattern/class/path, or inspect a specific asset. Use 'query' for search mode, 'asset_path' for inspect mode.

query.description: Search query (asset name pattern, supports * and ? wildcards). Triggers search mode.

class.description: Filter by asset class (e.g., Blueprint, StaticMesh, Material)

asset_path.description: Asset path to inspect (e.g., /Game/Data/DT_Items). Triggers inspect mode.

depth.description: Recursion depth for nested objects (default: 2, max: 5). For inspect mode.

include_defaults.description: Include properties with default/empty values (default: false)

row_filter.description: Filter DataTable rows by name (wildcards supported)

tools.name: query-datatable
tools.description: Query a DataTable with structured schema and row fields.

tools.name: get-asset-diff
tools.description: Get structured diff for binary Unreal assets (Blueprints, Materials, DataTables) against SCM base version. Returns JSON diff showing added/removed/modified elements. Supports Git and Perforce.

asset_path.description: Asset path (e.g., /Game/Blueprints/BP_Player) or absolute file path to the .uasset

base_revision.description: Base revision to compare against. Git: commit hash or 'HEAD' (default). Perforce: changelist number or '#have' (default).

path.description: Root path to scan. Defaults to /Game

limit.description: Maximum number of candidates to return

tools.name: get-class-hierarchy
tools.description: Browse class inheritance tree showing parent and child classes. Supports both C++ and Blueprint classes.

include_blueprints.description: Include Blueprint subclasses when showing children (default: true)

tools.description: Find references to assets, Blueprint variables, or nodes. Use type='asset' to find referencers, dependencies, or both for an asset, type='property' to find variable usages within a Blueprint, or type='node' to find node/function usages. asset_path can be a directory (e.g., /Game/Blueprints) to search all Blueprints recursively.

variable_name.description: Variable name to find usages of (required for type='property')

node_class.description: Node class name to search for (e.g., K2Node_CallFunction). For type='node'

function_name.description: Function name to filter CallFunction nodes. For type='node'

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 100)

path_filter.description: Optional path prefix filter applied to returned asset results

output_path.description: Optional JSON report output path for asset reference results

tools.name: inspect-widget-blueprint
tools.description: Inspect Widget Blueprint-specific data including widget hierarchy from WidgetTree, slot information (anchors, offsets, sizes), visibility settings, named slots, property bindings, and animations. Works only with Widget Blueprints (UserWidget subclasses).

named_only.description: Only include widgets marked as Blueprint variables in flat lists and filtered tree output

resolve_slots.description: Resolve and include slot layout information (default: true)

tools.name: create-widget-blueprint

tools.name: edit-widget-animation

actor_name.description: Actor name/label when editing a placed actor instance

component_name.description: Optional WidgetComponent name. Required if multiple WidgetComponents exist.

tools.name: get-logs
tools.description: Retrieve Unreal Engine output log entries for debugging. Supports filtering by category, severity level, and message content.

category.description: Filter by log category (e.g., 'LogUEBridgeMCP', 'LogTemp', or '*' for all). Supports wildcards.

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 100, max: 1000)

tools.name: manage-workflow-presets
tools.description: Manage project workflow presets stored under Config/UEBridgeMCP/WorkflowPresets with list, upsert, and delete operations.

tools.name: run-workflow-preset
tools.description: Expand or execute a project workflow preset, resolving built-in resources, prompts, default arguments, and sequential tool calls.

dry_run.description: Expand prompt/resources/tool plan without executing tools

stop_on_error.description: Stop execution when a tool call fails

tools.name: query-performance-report

rollback_on_error.description: Cancel the transaction on the first failure

tools.name: query-worldpartition-cells
tools.description: Query visible world partition runtime cells, including bounds, streaming status, actor count, and data layer names.

include_content_bounds.description: Include content bounds in each cell summary

tools.name: create-asset
tools.description: Create a new asset by class name. Supports any UObject type including Blueprint, Material, DataTable, DataAsset, etc. Use full class names (e.g., 'Blueprint', 'Material', 'DataAsset') or Blueprint class paths.

asset_class.description: Asset class name. Examples: 'Blueprint', 'Material', 'DataTable', 'DataAsset', 'WidgetBlueprint', 'AnimBlueprint', or Blueprint class paths like '/Game/MyDataAsset.MyDataAsset_C'

parent_class.description: Parent class for Blueprints (e.g., 'Actor', 'Character'). For AnimBlueprint, specify skeleton asset path.

tools.name: add-widget
tools.description: Add a widget to a WidgetBlueprint tree.

tools.name: add-datatable-row
tools.description: Add a new row to a DataTable.

row_data.description: Row data as JSON string with property names matching the row struct. Example: {"Name":"Value","Count":5}

fields.description: Structured row fields object. Preferred over row_data for new callers.

tools.name: query-statetree
tools.description: Query StateTree structure: states, transitions, tasks, evaluators, and parameters. Use 'include' parameter to select sections: 'states', 'transitions', 'tasks', 'evaluators', 'parameters', 'all'.

tools.name: add-statetree-state
tools.description: Add a new state to a StateTree asset. Specify the state name, type, and optionally a parent state to create hierarchical state machines.

parent_state.description: Name of the parent state (optional, creates as root state if not specified)

tools.description: Add a transition between states in a StateTree asset. Transitions define how the state machine moves from one state to another.

source_state.description: Name of the source state (where the transition starts)

target_state.description: Name of the target state (where the transition goes). Use 'Succeeded', 'Failed', 'Next', or a state name.

tools.name: add-statetree-task
tools.description: Add a task to a state in a StateTree asset. Tasks define the behavior executed when a state is active.

state_name.description: Name of the state to add the task to

action.description: Action: 'start', 'stop', 'pause', 'resume', 'get-state', or 'wait-for'. Note: start/stop/wait-for are non-blocking (fire-and-forget), use get-state to poll.

timeout.description: [start] Timeout in seconds to wait for PIE ready (default: 30)

tools.name: pie-input
tools.description: Simulate player input in PIE. Actions: 'key' (press/release key, supports duration hold + axis keys), 'action' (trigger input action), 'axis' (drive a named axis mapping like 'MoveForward'), 'move-to' (pathfind), 'look-at' (rotate to face target). Works in both viewport and new_window PIE modes via PlayerController::InputKey + FTSTicker re-injection.

axis_name.description: [axis] Input axis name (e.g., 'MoveForward', 'Turn'). Resolves to the first axis key bound to it, then holds it with a ticker so axis mappings are driven every frame.

value.description: [axis] Target axis value (-1.0 to 1.0). Pass 0 to release any currently held axis key.

pressed.description: [key/action] True for press, false for release (default: press then release)

duration.description: [key] Hold the key pressed for this many seconds, then auto-release (re-injects IE_Repeat + IE_Axis every tick so axis mappings like MoveForward are driven). 0 or omitted = single press+release.

amount.description: [key] For axis keys, the amount depressed (-1.0 to 1.0). Default 1.0 for normal keys.

target_actor.description: [look-at] Target actor name (alternative to target location)

contents.name: Performance Triage Guide
contents.description: Guide for editor and PIE performance triage and evidence capture.
contents.text:
# Performance Triage Guide

1. Record whether you are measuring the editor world or PIE.
2. Capture frame time, FPS, actor/object counts, and memory signals before deep investigation.
3. Pair numerical summaries with at least one viewport or log artifact when the issue is visual or intermittent.
4. Keep snapshots timestamped and comparable so repeated runs can be diffed.
5. Treat one snapshot as evidence, not proof; look for stable regressions across multiple captures.

content.text:
You are a world builder for Unreal Engine 5.7. Follow these conventions:

STYLE: realistic
SCALE: room

PLACEMENT RULES:
- UE uses centimeters. 1 unit = 1 cm. A door is ~200cm tall, ~100cm wide.
- Y-axis is right, X-axis is forward, Z-axis is up.
- Place floors at Z=0. Standard floor height is 300-400 cm.
- Use folders to organize actors (e.g., 'Geometry/Walls', 'Lighting/Main').
- Tag actors for easy filtering (e.g., 'exterior', 'interactive').

LIGHTING RULES:
- Start with a DirectionalLight for sun/moon (yaw ~300, pitch ~-45).
- Add a SkyLight for ambient. Set to 'Captured Scene' for reflections.
- Use PointLights for interiors (Intensity ~5-20, Attenuation ~500-1000).
- SpotLights for focused areas (Inner angle ~15, Outer ~30).

WORKFLOW:
1. First use list_actors/get_level_info to understand the current scene.
2. Create a blockout with simple geometry before detailing.
3. Set mobility to Static for baked lighting, Movable for dynamic.
4. Group related actors in folders for clean hierarchy.
5. Use take_screenshot to verify placement visually.
